t_ch: 无论见过还是没见过，长生种应该不会记得咱们短生种的脸——就像一枚水果，昨日鲜活饱满，今日皱缩干瘪，再过几日便是一抔土灰了。
t_en: Whether he did or did not, it isn't likely that a long-life species can remember a short-life species like us. We are like fruits that look fresh one day, shrunken and dry the next, and are reduced to dust in yet another few days.

t_ch: 仙舟人看我们，便像这座石雕看我一般。要不了多久，我就不在人世了。
t_en: The Xianzhou people regard us just like how this statue would regard me. It won't be long before I'm gone.

t_ch: 现任的龙尊就不长这样。
t_en: The current high elder doesn't look like this.

t_ch: 我知道。据说中间有些复杂的变故。可惜学会挖掘不出其中的秘密。
t_en: I know. Something complicated took place. It's a shame that the Guild is unable to unearth the secret.

t_ch: 好了吗，还要去下一处呢…
t_en: Are you done? We need to head to the next spot...

t_ch: 嘿，年轻人真是缺乏耐心。焦虑本该属于我这样时日无多的老头才对。
t_en: Tsk, youngsters nowadays are so impatient. Only dying old people like me should feel anxious.

t_ch: 咱们走吧，去持明卵那儿看看。
t_en: Let's go and check out the Vidyadhara eggs.

t_ch: 老人凑近珍珠般的持明卵，仔细观瞧其上的流光与鳞纹，浑然不知时间流逝。
t_en: The old man approaches the pearl-like Vidyadhara egg to examine the glimmer and scales on its surface. He is so captivated that he has lost track of time.

t_ch: 狐人虽然长寿，终究无法迈过死亡的大限。仙舟人拥有无尽形寿…却换来了疯狂。
t_en: The foxians have a long lifespan, but they aren't immortal. The Xianzhou people have an endless lifespan, but it has driven them to madness...

t_ch: 唯有持明族转世重生，轮回不息。
t_en: Only the Vidyadhara undergo rebirths and endless circles of life.

t_ch: 所以…总有「极限」无法被超越。这便是追寻永生会得到的必然结局吗？
t_en: So... there will always be an "insurmountable limit." Is this inevitable in the pursuit of immortality?

t_ch: 可我们这些朝生暮死的短生种，真的想永远永远活下去吗？…我们只是想多享受享受青春罢了。
t_en: Do we, short-life species, really want to live forever? We just want to stay youthful for as long as possible.

t_ch: 贝尼尼老师显然也是理解了这一点，才会留下那些图案的。
t_en: Bernini must have realized that. That is why he has left behind those drawings.

t_ch: 咱们该走了。
t_en: It's time to go.

t_ch: 要不要去别的地方瞧瞧？
t_en: Should we check out some other places?

t_ch: 陶德仿佛从睡梦中被人叫醒，他下意识地摸了摸自己的口袋，神色开始焦躁起来。
t_en: Todd seems like he had just been awakened from his sleep. Instinctively, he reaches for his pocket and immediately looks anxious.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我的笔记本…我的笔记本不见了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, my notebook... It's missing.

t_ch: 最后用上它是在哪儿？
t_en: Where did you last use it?

t_ch: 瞧瞧我，年纪大了脑子就不太灵光了。让我想想……
t_en: My memory is failing me because of old age. Let me think...

t_ch: 弄丢了？
t_en: You dropped it somewhere?

t_ch: 应该是吧？我也不确定，一定是我随手搁置。
t_en: I guess so? I'm not sure. I must have misplaced it.

t_ch: 好像在龙尊雕像那儿还用过它来着，我录了几笔，应该是随手放在那儿了。
t_en: I think I used it at the high elder statue. I wrote something down and put it down somewhere.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}，帮个忙吧，帮我回去找找我的笔记本吧。
t_en: {F#M'girl}{M#M'boy}, please do me a favor and help me look for my notebook.

t_ch: 抱歉，药力过去了，我的关节又开始疼起来了，好像有人撒了一蓬针在里面。我实在挪不动了。
t_en: I'm sorry. The effect of the pill is waning, and my joints are hurting like there are needles inside. I can't walk anymore.

t_ch: 呃…呜…骨头……
t_en: Urgh... Ow... My bones...

t_ch: 在满是皱纹的老脸上，陶德的五官紧紧皱缩了起来。
t_en: Todd's wrinkled face crinkles up in pain.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，行行好，帮老夫去拿回那本笔记好么？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, could you do me a favor and help me retrieve my notebook?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，怎么样，找到我的笔记本了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did you find my notebook?

t_ch: 一本笔记被弃置在龙尊雕像的底座附近。
t_en: A notebook is left near the base of the high elder statue.

t_ch: 捡起笔记本。
t_en: Pick up the notebook.

t_ch: （这是陶德的笔记本没错了，可里面的纸页基本都是空白的，并没有什么笔记啊，奇怪……）
t_en: (This is Todd's notebook, but it's empty inside. How strange...)

t_ch: 我找到你的笔记本了。
t_en: I found your notebook.

t_ch: 人老了…真是百无一用。
t_en: You really can't do much when you're getting old.

t_ch: 年轻时可以不远千万光年地跨越一颗又一颗星星，但眼前这几步路却像要穿过一座疼痛的地狱，向宇宙尽头跋涉。
t_en: When I was young, I could hop from star to star no matter the millions of light-years apart. Now, I feel like I'm going through the pain of hell and going on a journey to the end of the universe when taking just a few steps.

t_ch: 年轻时我总是享受孤独的研究和冒险，觉得自己无所不能。现在连一本笔记丢了，都要请求一位好心的年轻人施舍仁慈。
t_en: When I was young, I enjoyed taking solo adventures and trips and felt like there was nothing I couldn't do. Now, I have to beg for the kindness of a youngster to retrieve my lost notebook.

t_ch: 谢啦，{NICKNAME}…真是辛苦你了。
t_en: Thanks, {NICKNAME}... I owe you a big one.

t_ch: 笔记是空白的…
t_en: The notebook is empty...

t_ch: 陶德沉默了。
t_en: Todd falls silent.

t_ch: （陶德给的坐标显示那标识就在附近，可是这里也不见有。）
t_en: (The coordinates given by Todd show that the marking is nearby, but I can't find it here.)

t_ch: （保险起见，还是打开相机用侦测功能看一下吧？）
t_en: (Should I switch on the camera's detection system, just to be safe?)

t_ch: 开启「精准识别」模式。
t_en: Enable the Precise Recognition mode.

t_ch: 请注意，侦测到特定反应，附近存在学会通行光学标识，所属学士：贝尼尼•赛拉诺。
t_en: Note: Specific response detected. An optical marking of the guild is nearby. Owner: Scholar Bernini Serrano.

t_ch: （咦…这就是陶德口中的「标识」啊…这个图案有点意思，但怎么是不完整的？）
t_en: (Hm... Is this what Todd meant by marking? This drawing is interesting, but it isn't complete.)

t_ch: （可陶德给的坐标明明就在这里，残缺的标识估计就在这附近了，或许…要在这附近找找？）
t_en: (But the coordinate that Todd gave leads here. The missing markings are probably around somewhere... Maybe I should look around?)

t_ch: （好像也没发现什么特别的地方，还是开启侦测功能看看吧？）
t_en: (Nothing really looks out of place, maybe I should switch on the camera's detection system?)

t_ch: （成功了！果然如陶德所说…这玩意是光敏材料，只有在特定角度折射的光线下才能出现。）
t_en: (I got it! Just as Todd said... this thing is made of photosensitive material. It only appears under light refracted at specific angles.)

t_ch: 仿佛就在刚才，他又老了十岁，皮肤上每一道皱纹，那些像是永远擦不干净的污渍似的老人斑变得更加清晰可见。
t_en: He looks like he has aged another ten years in an instant. The wrinkles and the age spots that resemble stubborn stains on his face suddenly become more visible.

t_ch: 他望向你的眼神浑浊又疲惫，像无助的乞丐蜷缩在命运的死角里，恳求恩主施舍。
t_en: His eyes are bleak and weary when he gazes at you. He looks like a helpless beggar cowering in a dark corner, pleading for charity.

t_ch: 你读懂了这无声的哀求，他希望你保持沉默。
t_en: You can hear him silent imploring. He wishes for you to stay silent.

t_ch: （看了看他，什么也不说）
t_en: (You glance at him without making a comment.)

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。答应你的酬劳…我说话算话。
t_en: Thanks, {NICKNAME}. I'll pay you as promised.

t_ch: 至于知识，我可不会忘记博识学会的对等回报原则。不过还得再晚一些时候，待我将最近的研究成果进行一次总结，我再来和你分享。
t_en: As for the knowledge, I'll never forget the Intelligentsia Guild's rule of reciprocity. I'll summarize my recent research and share the results with you.

t_ch: 凭吊结束了，{NICKNAME}，咱们回去吧，好吗？
t_en: I'm done paying my respects. Should we go back, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 你和陶德返回了星槎海，一路上，你们再也没有交谈。
t_en: Todd and you return to Starskiff Haven. None of you have spoken during the journey.

t_ch: （陶德要找的持明卵…啧，不同持明卵之间的体积差距也太大了吧……）
t_en: (Todd is looking for a Vidyadhara egg. Those eggs differ so much in size...)

t_ch: （无法握住的光芒，却能被相机拍下来…陶德追寻的应该就是这玩意了……）
t_en: (The light is untouchable but can be captured by the camera... This should be the one Todd is looking for...)

t_ch: （陶德想看的是历史壁画，好像就在这附近……）
t_en: (The historical murals Todd is interested in seem to be nearby...)

t_ch: （陶德想看的持明卵，好像就在这附近……）
t_en: (The Vidyadhara egg Todd wants to see seems to be nearby...)

t_ch: （陶德想看的「龙形光芒」…这里视野开阔，正适合远眺，在这附近找找看吧……）
t_en: (Todd wants to see the dragon-shaped ray of light... This place has an unobstructed view. Let's look around here...)

t_ch: 请提交在丹鼎司找到的拓片
t_en: Please submit the rubbings found in the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 请提交在鳞渊境找到的拓片
t_en: Please submit the rubbings found in Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: （这些标识似乎都是残缺不全的，看来在附近仔细找找缺失的部分了。）
t_en: (These markings seem incomplete. I should probably look for the missing parts nearby.)

t_ch: （壮士挽弓，看上去似乎和仙舟历史有极强的关联，不知道这究竟是在讲述哪一段史实……）
t_en: (A warrior with a bow. It seems to have a strong association with Xianzhou's history, but I'm not sure which part it is referring to...)

t_ch: （这个图案…看着有点像什么巨木的树根，难道是…建木？）
t_en: (This drawing... looks like the roots of an enormous tree. Is it... the Ambrosial Arbor?)

t_ch: （图案上有几艘船，难道是…最初的几艘仙舟？）
t_en: (There are some ships in this drawing. Could they be... the first few Xianzhou ships?)

t_ch: （飞天的巨舟，仙舟人喜欢将宇宙和天空称为「天之外海」，还挺浪漫…只是旅程中有多少艰辛或许只有他们自己知道了……）
t_en: (A gigantic flying ship. The Xianzhou people like to call the universe the "seas beyond the sky." How romantic... Perhaps, they are the only ones who understand how arduous the journey through the universe is...)

t_ch: （这个图案的含义晦暗难明，看上去像是某种对抗，这人背后还有翅膀…这到底是什么玩意？）
t_en: (This drawing bears an obscured meaning. It seems to portray some kind of resistance, and there is a being with wings on its back... What does it mean?)

t_ch: （好邪恶的图案，光是远远地看一眼都好像会沾染上某种不幸…这是隐喻了什么吗？）
t_en: (What an evil drawing. It seems like one will be stricken with some misfortune just by looking at it... Is there an intrinsic meaning to it?)

t_ch: （五个人的剪影？不知这些人是谁……）
t_en: (Five silhouettes? I wonder who they are...)

t_ch: （而且…光线折射的角度会影响标识的拼合，届时还要调整一下相机的视角……）
t_en: (Also, the angle of refraction will affect the collocation of the marking. I'll have to adjust the camera angle...)

t_ch: 开启「精准识别」模式。
t_en: Enable the Precise Recognition mode.

t_ch: （…那里好像有光亮反应了？过去看看好了。）
t_en: (...Something's glimmering over there? It's worth going to take a look.)

t_ch: （这么说…只要将相机<color=#dbc291ff>调整到合适的角度</color>，就能将标识<color=#dbc291ff>拼合</color>在一起……）
t_en: (So... I just have to get the Camera <color=#dbc291ff>adjusted to the right angle</color> and I'll be able to get the markings <color=#dbc291ff>pieced together</color>...)

t_ch: （不过，这个标识…图案看上去像一棵树，又像是人，到底是什么玩意？树人？）
t_en: (This marking... looks half like a tree and half like a human. What exactly is it? A tree person?)

t_ch: 你隐隐觉得有些不对劲，你产生了一种奇怪的预感：陶德的事似乎并没有这么简单，你和他之间的纠缠也不该止步于此。
t_en: You sense something is off and a peculiar feeling of foreboding comes over you: Todd's issues don't seem to be as straightforward as they appear, and your entanglement with him shouldn't stop here.

t_ch: 不知陶德是否真的得到了自己想要的一切……
t_en: I wonder if Todd has truly gotten everything he wanted...

t_ch: 也许他得偿所愿，会就此离开。<color=#dbc291ff>也许明天他还会联系我</color>？谁知道呢……
t_en: Perhaps he got what he wanted and left. <color=#dbc291ff>Or maybe he'll contact me again tomorrow.</color> Who knows...

t_ch: 你来了啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Oh it's you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: …陶德？
t_en: ...Todd?

t_ch: 正是老夫，如假包换。
t_en: Same old me. The one and only.

t_ch: 现在用「老夫」已经不妥了，因为这个身体已经不再衰老了。
t_en: Although I probably shouldn't say "old," because this body doesn't grow old anymore.

t_ch: 你哪位？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我，陶德。如假包换。
t_en: It's me, Todd. The one and only.

t_ch: 这就是我想和你分享的，身为仙舟文化的研究者，我和我的老师探寻的众多知识中，最珍贵的部分已经被我掌握了。
t_en: This is what I want to share with you. As a researcher of Xianzhou culture, my teacher and I have combed through all the literature. And I've gotten a good grasp of all the main precious parts.

t_ch: 你变年轻了？！
t_en: Have you gotten younger!?

t_ch: 嘘——小点声！仙舟人对这样的事情分外警惕。
t_en: Hey — Shhh! The Xianzhou people are extremely sensitive to that kind of talk.

t_ch: 你整容啦？
t_en: Did you get plastic surgery?

t_ch: …不必胡乱猜想。
t_en: ...Now that's letting your imagination run wild.

t_ch: 长生不老…仙舟联盟拥有所有短生种梦寐以求的奥秘，但他们藏珍自重。
t_en: Immortality... The Xianzhou Alliance possesses a secret which all short-life species crave, but they keep it to themselves.

t_ch: 学会一直渴望通过与联盟打交道，获得稳定安全的长生疗法——整个仙舟学的起源便立足于此。
t_en: The Guild is always keen to obtain stable and safe longevity therapies from the Xianzhou Alliance — In fact, it forms the basis for all research into Xianzhou culture.

t_ch: 但一代又一代的学士来到此处，老去，却无功而返。到最后，学会几乎要放弃这项课题了。
t_en: But generation after generation of scholars came here, grew old, and returned without success. Until finally, the Guild almost gave up this field completely.

t_ch: 我的老师贝尼尼学士也是其中一人。他本该像所有人一样毫无收获。但他别有奇遇。
t_en: My teacher, Scholar Bernini, was one of them. He should have gone home empty-handed just like everyone else. But somehow what he found was adventure.

t_ch: 所以你想进入鳞渊境…
t_en: So you want to enter Scalegorge Waterscape...

t_ch: 留在鳞渊境里的那些图案…
t_en: Those patterns left in Scalegorge Waterscape...

t_ch: 是啊，我的老师被一位持明带进了圣地，因此将寻求长生的目光转向了持明。
t_en: Yes. A Vidyadhara brought my teacher to this sacred place once, which switched his focus on longevity over to the Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 但这个想法触犯了仙舟「十恶逆」的禁律，他知道自己不可以留下只言片语的线索，因此他使用了学会内部的标识，留下了众多看起来只是在描绘历史的图案。
t_en: But that violated the Xianzhou's "Ten Unpardonable Sins." Knowing he couldn't leave behind any traces, he used the Guild's markings and left behind a series of patterns that seemed to merely illustrate history.

t_ch: 而我，识别了这些图案内在的编码，也理解了图案的涵义——持明，持明才是寻求长生最正确的答案。
t_en: And now that I've deciphered the code to these patterns, I now understand their hidden meaning — the Vidyadhara pursue longevity in the correct manner.

t_ch: 你做了什么…
t_en: What did you do...

t_ch: 在仙舟，这就是犯罪。
t_en: That's a crime on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 陶德年轻昂扬的脸上露出一丝受伤的表情。
t_en: A look of pain falls across Todd's young face.

t_ch: 我从没想过，自己这辈子竟会和「犯罪」扯上关系…可是，{NICKNAME}，当意识到的时候，我已经这么做了。
t_en: I never thought I'd ever have anything to do with "crime" in my life, but... {NICKNAME}, by the time I'd realized it, things had already happened.

t_ch: 我提取了一些持明卵内部的生物样本，没有任何人因此受到伤害。
t_en: I took a few samples from inside a Vidyadhara egg. Nobody was harmed in the slightest.

t_ch: 那颗持明卵也保持完好，它只是失去了…一点儿「蛋清」。但它对人的造福却显而易见，不是吗？
t_en: That egg also remained intact, but it just lost... a little bit of "egg white." That's all. But its benefit to society is obvious, isn't it?

t_ch: 陶德抬起自己曾经干瘪枯瘦的手，那些皱纹已然消失，露出白皙光滑的肌肤。
t_en: Todd raised his once dry and skinny hand to show that his wrinkles had disappeared, revealing fair, smooth skin instead.

t_ch: 我本可以一走了之，我本可以保持沉默，一旦将这件事告知他人，我便要承受锒铛入狱的风险。但…对你，我还是想将这一切坦陈相告。
t_en: I could have just walked away. I could've just kept quiet — talking to anyone would mean risking jail time. But... It's you, and I wanted to tell you about all of this.

t_ch: 我一生恪守学会的对等回报原则，现在也是如此。若没有你的帮助，我绝对无法走到今天。
t_en: My whole life I've always been sticking to the rule of reciprocity, and nothing's changed now. Without your help, I would never be where I am today.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你是个出类拔萃的年轻人，而老夫…而我，动了收徒的念头。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you are an outstanding young {F#woman}{M#man}, and this old timer... Well, you've inspired me to reconsider taking on an apprentice.

t_ch: 我有个提议：加入学会，我们一起共享这秘密带来的荣誉、财富还有漫长的青春。你可以自由做任何你想做的事情。只要在我们离开罗浮前，守口如瓶。
t_en: I've got a proposal for you: Join our Guild! We'll split all the glory, the riches, and the extended youth granted by this secret. You'll be free to do whatever you want. Just keep this a secret until we leave the Luofu.

t_ch: 考虑一下如何？然后<color=#dbc291ff>给我一个答复</color>吧？
t_en: How about you think it over? And then <color=#dbc291ff>give me your answer</color>, okay?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，考虑的怎么样了？
t_en: Thought things through yet, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 作为帮助过我的恩人，我不会亏待你的。
t_en: As someone who's shown me kindness before, I will not treat you poorly.

t_ch: （拒绝陶德的提议）
t_en: (Decline Todd's proposal)

t_ch: 拒绝？为什么？
t_en: No? Why not?

t_ch: 我对长生不老没兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested in immortality.

t_ch: 哈…哈哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Ha... Hahaha...

t_ch: 你不明白，你根本不了解这个秘密的价值。你和年轻时的我一样轻狂自大，以为自己会有数不尽的可能，以为青春永不消逝。
t_en: You don't get it. You don't understand the true value of this secret at all. I was just a know-it-all just like you at your age, thinking I'd have countless possibilities before me and that youth would never fade.

t_ch: 不，你不懂！你根本不会相信、也不可能想象衰老是怎样一种地狱！
t_en: No, you don't understand! You could never believe or even imagine what kind of hell aging actually is!

t_ch: 你不知道被失禁的屈辱浸透的滋味，不用被关节炎的刺痛反复折磨，遗忘和失智还没有将你敏锐的脑瓜啃得千疮百孔……
t_en: You don't know what it's like to be soaked in the humiliation of your own incontinence. The never-ending torture of arthritis. Memory loss and dementia have yet to gnaw into your quick-witted mind just yet...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…总有一天，我们都会变老的。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... One day, we'll all be old.

t_ch: 我不想成为罪犯的帮凶。
t_en: I have no intention of becoming a criminal accomplice.

t_ch: 你害怕？你害怕被仙舟联盟狩猎？
t_en: You're scared? Are you scared of being hunted by the Xianzhou Alliance?

t_ch: 不必惊慌，当巨富和掌权者都能从中获益时，你会有无数的保护伞。仙舟将对你无可奈何。
t_en: Don't panic. You'll find you have plenty of protection when the super-rich and powerful can profit from something. The Xianzhou will be helpless.

t_ch: 你有某种与生俱来的正义感？还是你相信了仙舟人禁绝丰饶的那套说辞？唉，这都不重要了。
t_en: Do you have some sense of innate justice? Or do you believe that rhetoric about how the Xianzhou people forbid Abundance? *sigh* None of that matters now, anyway.

t_ch: 陶德深深叹了一口气。
t_en: Todd sighs deeply.

t_ch: 既然你已作出了选择，我想为了安全起见，我该离开了。
t_en: You've made your choice, I guess for the sake of my own safety, it's time for me to go.

t_ch: （答应守口如瓶）
t_en: (Promise to keep it secret)

t_ch: 我就知道我没看错人。
t_en: I knew I was right about you.

t_ch: 我不打算成为学徒…
t_en: I don't plan on becoming an apprentice...

t_ch: 无名客那艰辛的道路对年轻更有吸引力吗？我不勉强你。
t_en: Is the path of the Nameless more popular with the kids these days? I won't force things.

t_ch: 我对长生没有兴趣…
t_en: I'm not interested in immortality...

t_ch: 哈…哈哈哈哈哈……
t_en: Ha... Hahaha...

t_ch: 你不明白，你根本不了解这个秘密的价值。你和年轻时的我一样轻狂自大，以为自己会有数不尽的可能，以为青春永不消逝。
t_en: You don't get it. You don't understand the true value of this secret at all. I was just a know-it-all just like you at your age, thinking I'd have countless possibilities before me and that youth would never fade.

t_ch: 如果你后悔了，随时可以联系我，我的提议永远有效。
t_en: If you ever change your mind, come find me any time. My proposal stands in perpetuity.

t_ch: 陶德动作流畅地冲你欠身行礼，迅速转身离去。
t_en: Todd bows and shoots you a smooth salute, before turning to quickly leave.

t_ch: （检举陶德）
t_en: (Report Todd)

t_ch: 大毫耐心听完你的讲述，面不改色。
t_en: Dahao patiently listens to you recount the story, expressionless the whole time.

t_ch: 啧，总有不肯死心的学会探子认定联盟的禁忌只是自私自利的谎言……
t_en: Tsk, there's always a Guild scout who reckons that the Alliance's taboos are just hypocritical lies and not for them...

t_ch: 「贪取不死」，这条十恶逆的禁律是绝对不容触碰的。我会将事情呈报给十王司的。谢啦，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: "Immortality Theft" is one of the most grievous of felonies and is absolutely unpardonable. I'll report this to the Ten-Lords Commission. Thanks, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这位陶德先生的入关文牒，客栈的登记信息，在地衡司的案牍库中都有录入，不管他接下来拥有多少青春，他恐怕都得在幽囚狱里度过了。
t_en: Mr. Todd's customs clearance docket and check-in details from the inn have been saved to the document office at the Realm-Keeping Commission. No matter how many more years his newfound youth affords him, he'll be spending the majority of it behind bars at The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 十王司勾摄人犯，旁人不得干扰。
t_en: The Ten Lords are responsible for trying criminals, and bystanders cannot interfere.

t_ch: 你，我认得你，你是景元将军请来的客人。
t_en: You, I recognize you. Aren't you General Jing Yuan's esteemed guest?

t_ch: （提及陶德）
t_en: (Bring up Todd)

t_ch: 他显然不会再回住处了，吾等正在追索其踪迹。
t_en: He obviously won't be heading back to his residence. My team is on his tail.

t_ch: 天网恢恢，疏而不漏。他逃不了多久的。
t_en: You can't run from the long arm of the law. He won't get far.

t_ch: （转身离开）
t_en: (Turn and leave)

t_ch: 一个孩子孤零零地站在海边，不知为何，他稚嫩的面庞有些眼熟。
t_en: A child stands alone by the sea and, for some reason, his young face looks rather familiar.

t_ch: 你来了啊。
t_en: There you are.

t_ch: 我刚刚在<color=#dbc291ff>龙尊雕像</color>那儿等了一会儿。有个一言不发的人一直看着这里，我以为是十王司的人，就先躲到这儿了。
t_en: I was just hanging out over at the <color=#dbc291ff>high elder statue</color> and there was this guy, completely silent, who couldn't take his eyes off me. I thought he could be from the Ten-Lords Commission, so I snuck over here.

t_ch: 你怎么变成这副模样了？
t_en: How did you turn into... this?

t_ch: 到底发生什么事了？
t_en: What in the world happened?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，一次草率的实验，一个必然的结果。
t_en: Hehe, I just was a little sloppy with my experiment. An inevitable result.

t_ch: 我本来以为自己已经获得「长生」了，可惜事情并没有这么简单。
t_en: I thought I'd gained immortality, but unfortunately things were not that simple.

t_ch: 我的体形在不断缩小。皮肤、肌肉、内脏…我确实越来越年轻了，只是这份「青春」似乎没有尽头。
t_en: My body, it keeps shrinking. My skin, muscles, organs... Indeed, I'm getting younger, you see, but this "youth" doesn't seem to have an off switch.

t_ch: 返老还童？
t_en: You're de-aging?

t_ch: 有点像持明族的蜕生…
t_en: Kind of like the Vidyadhara tradition of hatching rebirth...

t_ch: 是啊，不过据我了解，他们的「蜕生」是有极限的。
t_en: Yes. But so far as I know, their "rebirths" have a limit.

t_ch: 从测试结果看，这个逆向生长的过程不会停止。要不了多久，我就会再也认不得字，看不懂自己写的报告，这和老年痴呆似乎没什么不同。
t_en: Test results indicate that this de-aging process seems to have no endpoint. It won't be long before I'm illiterate and won't be able to understand my own reports — which would seem no difference from regular dementia.

t_ch: 最终我也许会像持明一样变成一枚卵，也许会重新成为人类的胚胎——毕竟我并不是持明族。
t_en: In the end, I might even become an egg again, just like the Vidyadhara. Perhaps I could become a human embryo once again — I mean, I'm not Vidyadhara, am I?

t_ch: 啊…真是个讽刺的结果，不是吗？
t_en: Ahh... Isn't it ironic? Don't you think?

t_ch: 我实现了愿望，只是它不是以我所想的形式。人类最古老的故事都是这么个调调。现在看来，这些陈腔滥调也不无道理。
t_en: My wish came true — just not in the form I thought it would take. The oldest stories in human civilization are fables that take this line. Seems like they aren't just a cliche, after all.

t_ch: 需要我帮你做些什么？
t_en: What can I help you with?

t_ch: 不不，这回不用了。你已经为我做了很多很多了。比我的老师和弟子们加起来都多。而我已不需要再麻烦任何人了。
t_en: No no, no need this time. You've already done so much for me. More than my teachers and apprentices combined. And I don't want to bother anyone else anymore.

t_ch: 需要我请丹鼎司的医生吗？
t_en: Do you want me to fetch a healer from the Alchemy Commission?

t_ch: 小陶德的眼神里闪过一丝与年龄不符的傲慢。
t_en: Todd's little eyes lit up with a flash of arrogance that was beyond his years.

t_ch: 你的眼神…你在怜悯我？
t_en: That look in your eyes... Are you pitying me?

t_ch: 拜托，{NICKNAME}，什么都不用做。你能来和我聊聊，我已经很高兴了。
t_en: Please, {NICKNAME}. There really is no need for anything. Just coming to chat with me is cause enough for celebration.

t_ch: 我这一生比大部分不知自己所求，又无法得偿所愿的人强太多了。我著书立说，我授徒无数。虽然没有孩子，但我的学生中有不少让我颇为骄傲。
t_en: In this life I've been far better off than most people, who don't even know what they want, let alone how to get it. I became a published author, and I've taken on countless apprentices. I may have no children, but there are several of my students of whom I'm rather proud.

t_ch: 最重要的是，我活着完成了自己的研究结果——虽然它更像一个恶劣的玩笑。但是…够了，够了，我很满足了。
t_en: Best of all, I lived to see the results of my research — despite it all turning into a nasty joke. But... Enough, that's enough for now. I'm satisfied.

t_ch: 孩子苹果般的脸蛋似乎又小了一圈。
t_en: The child's cherub face seemed to be smaller yet again.

t_ch: …抱歉，之前对你说了很过分的话。
t_en: ...Sorry. That was a lot to just dump on you.

t_ch: 我只是…想向你道声谢，{NICKNAME}，自始至终你都从旁见证着我。
t_en: I just wanted to... say thanks to you, {NICKNAME}, for being there for me right from the start.

t_ch: 对了，我把自己的实验数据都记录下来了，想怎么处理，转交给我的弟子也好，销毁它也罢，甚至是给仙舟人…一切都由你处置。
t_en: By the way, I've recorded all the data from my experiments here — whether you want to pass it on to my apprentices, destroy it, or even hand it over to the Xianzhou authorities... The choice is yours.

t_ch: 再见了，我的朋友。
t_en: Goodbye, my friend.

t_ch: 关于「龙女大人」的事？
t_en: It's about the Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 是呀，我寻摸着{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}身手不凡，又帮龙女大人解过围，深得她的信任……
t_en: Yes, you have extraordinary skills, and you earned her trust when you rescued her...

t_ch: 深得信任？什么时候的事？
t_en: Earned her trust? When did that happen?

t_ch: 你在魔阴身手下救过她嘛。这也算是过命的交情了。
t_en: You rescued her from the mara-struck... I'd say she's forever in your debt.

t_ch: 若由你去见她，她多半就不会一溜烟跑了。
t_en: If you go see her, I'm sure she won't run away.

t_ch: 你们怎么老是让我跑腿啊？
t_en: Why do people here keep making me run errands?

t_ch: 若不是手里没几个人，我也不至于要求告外援。不过，这件事确实勉强算得上跑腿。
t_en: If I had more people, I wouldn't have to ask for external help. But as things stand...

t_ch: 我答应了！
t_en: Okay, I'll do it!

t_ch: 嚯，这么爽快！好啊，大毫在此拜谢恩公了。
t_en: Hah, that was fast! Thanks for your help.

t_ch: 是这样的，持明族的龙女，白露大人，又从丹鼎司逃出来了。之所以说「又」，是因为这位龙女性子跳脱，她出逃一事，地衡司早就司空见惯。
t_en: Here's the situation: Lady Bailu, the Vidyadhara Dragon Lady, has escaped from the Alchemy Commission again. I say "again" because the Realm-Keeping Commission has seen more than a few of her bad-tempered escapes.

t_ch: 持明族？
t_en: Vidyadhara?

t_ch: 你头一回抵达仙舟吧？那解释起来可麻烦了。持明族是创建仙舟联盟的三大种族之一。
t_en: This must be your first time on the Xianzhou... It's a long story. The Vidyadhara is one of the three races that established the Xianzhou Alliance.

t_ch: 他们的血脉传承与我们仙舟人不同。持明具有某种与生俱来的生命力，据说那是「不朽」的遗赠。
t_en: Their bloodline is different from us Xianzhou natives. The Vidyadhara are born with great vitality, the gift of the Permanence, as they say.

t_ch: 旁的就不说了，这些和今天的话题没关系。
t_en: That's all you need to know. The rest is irrelevant to what's happening today.

t_ch: 龙女是什么职位？
t_en: What does a Dragon Lady do exactly?

t_ch: 龙女…不是什么职位。只是大部分人对那位小姐的爱称罢了。
t_en: A Dragon Lady... isn't an official position. People address her that way out of love.

t_ch: 持明族设有「龙尊」的称号代代相传，地位形同族长。不过那是只有身具龙相，传承龙心的持明人才能接任的。
t_en: The Vidyadhara "high elder," on the other hand, is a position similar to a chief — it's been passed down from generation to generation. But only the Vidyadhara that possess draconic features and inherit the dragon heart can be successors.

t_ch: 白露小姐，便是罗浮上持明族未来的龙尊。
t_en: And Miss Bailu is the Vidyadhara's future high elder on the Luofu.

t_ch: 传闻她有仅凭触摸和垂泪就能治愈伤患的天分。其后更是拜入丹鼎司研习医道，所以大家也都尊称她为「衔药龙女」。
t_en: The rumor has always been that she can heal the wounded merely by touching them and shedding tears. She also studied medicine in the Alchemy Commission. That's why her other title is "Healer Lady."

t_ch: 逃出来？有谁关着她吗？
t_en: She escaped? Was she imprisoned?

t_ch: 也不能说是关。持明族的风俗与仙舟人总有些不同。白露天赋异禀，又是要接掌持明的人选，族中把她看得紧一些也是常事。
t_en: Not imprisoned exactly... Vidyadhara customs are somewhat different from Xianzhou native customs. Miss Bailu has incredible talents and is next in line to rule over the Vidyadhara — it's normal that her people would want to keep a close eye on her.

t_ch: 不过这位白露小姐的性子可不是一般的跳脱，总能找准时机从自己的潜邸里溜出来。
t_en: However, she also has a somewhat free spirit... She always manages to find a way to escape her residence.

t_ch: 您接着说。
t_en: I'm listening.

t_ch: 之前龙女翘家，都由我们地衡司负责盯梢，派人将她抓住送回丹鼎司。
t_en: It used to be the duty of the Realm-Keeping Commission to go after her and take her back to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 不过，这次情况不太一样，星核灾变一生，人力问题就成了无底窟窿。而且，就算我们抓住龙女大人，也暂时没法送她回去。
t_en: But, this time is different. Because of the Stellaron crisis, we're constantly short of manpower. Besides, even if we catch the Dragon Lady, we can't take her back anytime soon.

t_ch: 不知道你听说了没有，有人封闭了与丹鼎司有关的几个洞天。
t_en: I don't know if you heard — someone locked down the delves connected to the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 现在那里没人能进去，也没有人出来，可以说是音信全无。依兄弟愚见，我在其中嗅出了阴谋的味道。
t_en: Now nobody can get in or out — it's completely cut off. Something very fishy is going on.

t_ch: 那这龙女是怎么出来的？
t_en: So how did the Dragon Lady get out?

t_ch: 这也是我好奇的地方，她多半是乘着洞天闭锁之前溜了出来。也许她知道丹鼎司里发生了什么……
t_en: That's what I want to know. I guess she sneaked out before the delves were locked down. She might even know what happened in the Alchemy Commission...

t_ch: 我明明看见了街上有医士…
t_en: But I saw doctors...

t_ch: 那些医士都是派驻在其他洞天的医馆里。我已询问过情况了，他们对发生的事情一无所知。
t_en: Those doctors came from apothecaries in other delves. I've asked them, and they don't have the slightest clue what's going on.

t_ch: 那…我能做什么？
t_en: Then... is there really anything I can do?

t_ch: 我想拜托你两件事：第一是找到龙女大人，在丹鼎司洞天解除封锁之前，照顾好她，别让她跑丢了就行。
t_en: I'd like you to help with two things. First, find the Dragon Lady and take care of her until the Alchemy Commission delves are reopened. Don't lose sight of her.

t_ch: 第二件事也许有点难理解…希望能让她别再治疗魔阴身了。
t_en: The second thing might be a bit hard to understand... I want you to stop her from treating the mara-struck.

t_ch: 地衡司不能照顾她吗？
t_en: Can't the Realm-Keeping Commission take care of her?

t_ch: 这不是人手不足嘛。龙女大人狡猾得很，让她乖乖待在地衡司公廨里，肯定会趁我们不注意的时候偷溜出去，毕竟，这种事情也不是第一回了。
t_en: Like I said — we don't have enough people! The Dragon Lady is smart. If we try to keep her in the Realm-Keeping Commission Chancery, she'll sneak out the first chance she gets. Trust me, it's happened before...

t_ch: 何况翘家出逃算什么罪过，她的身份地位摆在那，我们还能把她关进牢里不成？这事儿只好拜托给你啦。
t_en: Besides, it isn't a crime for her to run away from her residence. And even if it were, given her status, we couldn't exactly stick her in jail and throw away the key! You're the only person we can turn to.

t_ch: 为什么不能治疗魔阴身？
t_en: Why stop her from treating the mara-struck?

t_ch: 魔阴身并非「疾病」。即便是衔药龙女，也只能稍缓其进程。
t_en: Being stricken with mara isn't like catching a curable disease. All the Healer Lady can do is slow the process.

t_ch: 魔阴身不是一种疾病？
t_en: It's not a disease?

t_ch: 拿你们短生种来说吧，你们会将「衰老」看作是疾病吗？
t_en: Take you short-life species as an example. Do you consider aging a disease?

t_ch: 衰老可以被延缓，但终有一天会到来。对仙舟人来说，魔阴身也是如此，只是它比死亡可怕得多。魔阴身是仙舟人血脉中无法回避的诅咒。
t_en: Aging can be slowed, but it's still unavoidable — just like being stricken with mara for the people of the Xianzhou... Unfortunately, becoming mara-struck is more terrifying than death. It's an inevitable curse in the blood of Xianzhou natives.

t_ch: 那魔阴身到底该怎么办？
t_en: Then, what can be done for them?

t_ch: 仙舟早有应对手段。魔阴身即将发作时，自有十王司的冥差、判官接引——总之，这不是丹鼎司应该插手的事情。
t_en: The Xianzhou has long had other methods. When mara is about to strike, the spiritfarers and judges of the Ten-Lords Commission promptly take the afflicted away. It's not something the Alchemy Commission should be meddling in.

t_ch: 再说了，魔阴身固然可怕，只要控制得当，并不会造成什么危害。长乐天这里有地衡司与云骑军，足以应对意外。
t_en: Mara is scary, but it won't cause any harm if you have it under control. We have the Realm-Keeping Commission and Cloud Knights here in Exalting Sanctum — more than enough to handle any eventuality.

t_ch: 龙女大人暂时压抑了魔阴身发作，也就意味十王司可能忽略那些人。让龙女大人治疗下去，无异于放任「魔阴身」流窜市井，制造恐慌。
t_en: However, the Dragon Lady's mara-suppression could lead to mara-struck falling under the Ten-Lords Commission's radar. If we let her carry on, we'll have mara-struck on every street corner — people will panic!

t_ch: 现在突发危机，魔阴身的处置方式更该谨慎，不能儿戏。
t_en: Seeing as we're already dealing with one crisis, we need to be extra vigilant when dealing with the mara-struck. This is not something that can be taken lightly.

t_ch: 明白了，交给我吧。
t_en: I see. Leave it to me.

t_ch: 你是景元将军和符玄大人的贵客，地衡司对你自然是很信任的。
t_en: You're the distinguished guest of General Jing Yuan and Diviner Fu Xuan. As such, you also have the full trust of the Realm-Keeping Commission.

t_ch: 恕兄弟我多嘴一句，如今的丹鼎司十分可疑，如果你遇上他们的人想带走龙女，最好留个心眼。
t_en: Oh, one more thing... There's a lot of suspicion surrounding the Alchemy Commission these days. If you discover someone from the commission on the hunt for the Dragon Lady, watch your step.

t_ch: 小事一桩。
t_en: It'll be a piece of cake.

t_ch: 爽快。
t_en: Great!

t_ch: 我最讨厌带小孩儿了。
t_en: Babysitting? No thanks, I'd rather watch paint dry.

t_ch: 这也是无奈之举，地衡司会重金感谢你的帮助。
t_en: There's no other way. The Realm-Keeping Commission will pay you handsomely for your help.

t_ch: 总感觉没有这么简单。
t_en: I have a feeling this is more complicated than it sounds...

t_ch: 你别多虑。看顾孩子确实是桩烦心事，但一定难不倒将军大人的贵客。
t_en: Bah, don't fret. Looking after children is a pain, but as the general's distinguished guest, I know you'll do great.

t_ch: 白露大人，我都说了这一趟你要听我的安排。你施展能力时，太引人瞩目了，我们还有正事要办啊。
t_en: Lady Bailu, I told you to follow my plan — you draw attention when you use your powers... We still have to take care of the matter at hand.

t_ch: 本小姐只是治病救人嘛，再说你刚才也不在…你刚才到哪儿去啦？
t_en: I was only tending to the sick! Besides, you weren't even here just now... Where did you go?

t_ch: 我这身体的情况，白露大人你最清楚了。广场上要是有十王司的人，我可就……
t_en: You know full well about my situation, Lady Bailu. If there are people from the Ten-Lords Commission present in the plaza, then—

t_ch: 你好，十王司来抓你了。
t_en: Hello, Ten-Lords Commission here, you're under arrest.

t_ch: 十、十王司？！
t_en: Ah! The T—Ten-Lords Commission!?

t_ch: …你根本就不是十王司的冥差，别在这平白无故地吓人。
t_en: ...You're not a Ten-Lords spiritfarer — why are you trying to frighten people!?

t_ch: 龙女大人？可算是找到你了。
t_en: The Dragon Lady? I finally found you.

t_ch: 你、你是谁？！
t_en: Wh—Who are you!?

t_ch: …你不是十王司的冥差。呼，别在这平白无故地吓人哪。
t_en: ...You're not a Ten-Lords spiritfarer — why are you trying to frighten people!?

t_ch: 本小姐这次出逃…离开丹鼎司，是官方认证的「外出问诊」！不不不是擅自出逃！这位医护姐姐可以为我作证！
t_en: Hmph, I only escaped — I—I mean LEFT the Alchemy Commission for an official medical visit! There's nothing more to it, okay!? The nurse here can vouch for me!

t_ch: 白露大人别慌张，你看清了，{F#她}{M#他}也不是丹鼎司的人。
t_en: Easy, Lady Bailu — {F#she's}{M#he's} clearly not from the Alchemy Commission either.

t_ch: 可恶，别吓我呀…啊，等等，我见过你。
t_en: Ugh, then don't scare me like that... Ah, wait a minute — I've seen you before.

t_ch: 在刚才那个广场上，我被一群魔阴身包围了，是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}出手把他们全都摆平了，对吧？我记得是你！你来得正好。
t_en: In the plaza — I was surrounded by all those mara-struck... You rescued me, right? It was you! You came just in time.

t_ch: 半夏，你不是不知道接下来怎么走吗，{F#她}{M#他}身体结实，武艺又好，肯定帮得上忙。
t_en: Banxia, you don't know the way from here, right? This {F#girl's}{M#guy's} no pushover — {F#she'll}{M#he'll} be able to help.

t_ch: 不行啊，白露大人，我信不过{F#她}{M#他}……
t_en: No, Lady Bailu. I don't trust {F#her}{M#him}...

t_ch: 您请回吧。
t_en: Please, leave us.

t_ch: 这可由不得你们。
t_en: I'm afraid that's not your call to make.

t_ch: 白露大人有我照顾，不劳你费心。
t_en: I'm taking care of Lady Bailu. You don't need to worry.

t_ch: 我是受地衡司之托来找白露的。
t_en: I was asked by the Realm-Keeping Commission to find Bailu.

t_ch: 告诉地衡司的人，白露大人由我们丹鼎司来照顾就行，别多管闲事。
t_en: Tell the Realm-Keeping Commission that Lady Bailu is in the Alchemy Commission's care... and not to meddle in other people's affairs.

t_ch: 我是整个银河最值得信任的人。
t_en: I'm the most trustworthy person in the whole cosmos.

t_ch: 通常说这种话的人都不值得信任。
t_en: ...Said no trustworthy person ever.

t_ch: 你们两个先别吵了，那边好像是丹鼎司的人！
t_en: You two, stop arguing! I think there's someone from the Alchemy Commission over there...

t_ch: 你先帮我们藏起来！事情等会儿再说！
t_en: You — help us hide first! We'll talk later!

t_ch: 这个房间看上去是能够暂时隐藏住白露她们两人的。
t_en: This room seems to be able to hide them for a moment.

t_ch: 白露大人，这边这边。
t_en: Lady Bailu, this way.

t_ch: 来了！
t_en: Coming!

t_ch: 你快去帮我们把那个人支开，拜托了！有话我们一会儿慢慢说。
t_en: Help us get rid of him, please! We'll have plenty of time to talk later.

t_ch: 白露和半夏两人躲进了房间里压低了声音，一般人很难发现她们。
t_en: Bailu and Banxia hid in the room and lowered their voices. They should be hard to discover.

t_ch: （总之，先去问问，再决定该怎么办吧。）
t_en: (Hmm... I'll ask first and then decide what to do.)

t_ch: 干脆假装是站在牌桌后指点江山的牌友吧，只要双手抱胸、时不时摇摇头，就能顺利融入环境气氛之中…但是这根本称不上是藏身处，再去找找还有没有更合适的地方吧。
t_en: Of course, you can pretend to be a player and stand beside a game table, commenting on other players' moves with some head-shaking and finger-pointing. However, it's not a proper hiding spot. You shall find a better place to hide.

t_ch: 如果你和医护都像白露一样身高吃亏，那么这座石造景观后面也许是个不错的藏身处。再去找找还有没有更合适的地方吧。
t_en: If you and the nurse are both as short as Bailu, then this stone design would be enough to hide you. You shall find a better place to hide.

t_ch: 这位{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}请留步，我想问你一点儿事情。
t_en: You there, excuse me. I want to ask you something.

t_ch: 有话快说，我赶时间。
t_en: Make it quick, I'm in a hurry.

t_ch: 别急别急，我比你还急，你先听我问完好吧？
t_en: Hmph, you're not the only one. Can you at least hear me out first?

t_ch: 我什么都不知道，别问我。
t_en: You came to the wrong person — I don't know anything.

t_ch: 我还没有问呢，你先听我问完好吧？
t_en: I haven't asked anything yet. At least hear me out first?

t_ch: 请问你是否在找一位小妹妹？
t_en: Are you looking for a little girl?

t_ch: 什么小妹妹？你误会了，我确实在找人没错，你先听我把话说完。
t_en: Little girl? You've got the wrong person. I am looking for someone, though...

t_ch: 有一位身着丹鼎司衣装的女性，跟你差不多高，好像是朝着渡口的那个方向过去了。你有没有瞧见呢？
t_en: Have you seen a woman wearing an Alchemy Commission uniform? She's about the same height as you — I think she was heading towards the jetty.

t_ch: 我和她是同道，但她却好像一直躲着我，嘿嘿……
t_en: We walk the same path, but it would seem she's hiding from me, hehe...

t_ch: 我没有看见。
t_en: I haven't seen anyone like that.

t_ch: 是吗，谢谢你，那我去渡口那边找找。
t_en: Very well, thank you. I'll head over to the jetty.

t_ch: 那边没有你要找的人。
t_en: You won't find the person you're looking for there.

t_ch: 那边没有我要找的人？
t_en: And why's that?

t_ch: 现在你又知道我要找的是什么人了。我反倒要问问，你是什么人？
t_en: ...Say, now that you know who I'm looking for, why don't you tell me who you are?

t_ch: …我其实看见了，在相反的方向。
t_en: ...Come to think of it, I did see her — she was going in the opposite direction.

t_ch: 相反的方向？不对吧，我就是从反方向过来的。
t_en: The opposite direction? It can't be. I just came from that direction.

t_ch: 你不会是帮她遮掩吧，看来你知道的不少，我得好好问问。
t_en: Are you covering for her? You know something alright — I have a few more questions for you.

t_ch: 你也是从丹鼎司洞天过来的？
t_en: Did you come from the Alchemy Commission delves?

t_ch: …这话怎么说？
t_en: ...What makes you say that?

t_ch: 我刚从丹鼎司过来。
t_en: I just came from the Alchemy Commission.

t_ch: 什么？丹鼎司洞天早已封锁，若不是药王的莳者……
t_en: What? The Alchemy Commission delves were locked down ages ago. If you aren't a disciple, then...

t_ch: 你是在套我的话吧，你说你是从丹鼎司洞天过来的，证据呢？
t_en: You're trying to get me to say something, aren't you? You said you came from the Alchemy Commission. Where's your proof?

t_ch: 你是怎么出来的，我也是。
t_en: I came here the same way you did.

t_ch: 啧，把别人当傻子糊弄很过瘾么？
t_en: Tsk, you think this is a joke?

t_ch: 我是景元将军的客人，懂了吧？
t_en: I'm General Jing Yuan's guest. Understand?

t_ch: 景元？我明白啦，那就是将军的狗了。
t_en: Jing Yuan? I understand. That makes you a general's lackey.

t_ch: 我看你就是在包庇那个女人，看来你知道的不少，我得好好问问。
t_en: You're covering for that woman — it's obvious. I'm not done questioning you...

t_ch: 我也是来抓她的。
t_en: I'm here to catch her as well.

t_ch: 哦，你也是？
t_en: ...You too?

t_ch: 「药王慈怀，建木生发……」
t_en: "Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, the Arbor's new growth..."

t_ch: 你突然说这谁懂啊？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 对不上暗号，也敢自称与我同事。
t_en: You claim to walk the same path, yet you don't even know the code.

t_ch: 看来你知道的不少，我得好好问问。
t_en: Still, it's clear you know something... I'm not done questioning you.

t_ch: 药王慈怀，建木生发。
t_en: Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, the Arbor's new growth.

t_ch: 你为什么又把这句口号重复了一遍？你听说过哪个组织的接头暗号是一模一样的……
t_en: Ha, repeating it back to me? What kind of organization would devise a code like that...?

t_ch: 你听过哪个组织的接头暗号是一模一样的……
t_en: What kind of organization would devise a code like that...?

t_ch: 看来你知道的不少，我得好好问问。
t_en: Still, it's clear you know something... I'm not done questioning you.

t_ch: 药王怀慈，长生不老！
t_en: Clemency of Sanctus Medicus, the Eternity's pure birth!

t_ch: 魁首说过很多次，把「慈怀」说成「怀慈」的，定斩不赦。
t_en: The master said many times that anyone using "clemency" instead of "mercy" should be killed without exception.

t_ch: 看来你知道的不少，我得好好问问。
t_en: Still, it's clear you know something... I'm not done questioning you.

t_ch: 莳者一心，同登仙道。
t_en: Unity of Discipuli Medici, the Immortality's true path.

t_ch: …要是这都能蒙对的话，我就无话可说了。
t_en: ...Hm, not something you could've guessed.

t_ch: 看来你我同为莳者，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}。
t_en: Good to be acquainted with another disciple, {F#sister}{M#brother}.

t_ch: 事关泄密，我把上面的指令交给你，你看了指示便会明白。
t_en: There's been a leak. Here are the orders — read them and you'll understand.

t_ch: 抓住叛徒要紧，我先去另一个方向搜索了。
t_en: We must catch the traitor. I'll go search in another direction.

t_ch: （这个丹鼎司的医士不太对劲，应该仔细调查一下。）
t_en: (There's more to this Alchemy Commission doctor than meets the eye. I wonder if I can glean more clues from him.)

t_ch: （好像还有些重要线索错过了，应该再仔细调查一下。）
t_en: (Feels like I'm still missing an important clue... more investigation needed.)

t_ch: （仅从穿着上来看，这人是丹鼎司的医士。）
t_en: (That's an Alchemy Commission doctor's uniform alright.)

t_ch: （也许他就是大毫说的「可疑」之人…）
t_en: (Dahao did say there was suspicion surrounding the Alchemy Commission...)

t_ch: （翻翻他的裤兜…）
t_en: (Let's check his pants pockets...)

t_ch: 太多人喜欢把秘密揉作一团，揣进裤兜。
t_en: Secrets are often scrunched up and shoved into pockets — why not have a look?

t_ch: （前侧裤兜）
t_en: (Pants pockets at front...)

t_ch: 一只最新款的玉兆手镯，不能解锁；还有一枚金色的徽章。
t_en: A jade abacus bracelet with the latest design that cannot be unlocked, with a Golden Badge.

t_ch: 先拿走吧。
t_en: I'd better take this.

t_ch: （后侧裤兜）
t_en: (Pants pockets at back...)

t_ch: 一张折叠的信纸，上面写着「药王慈怀，建木生发。莳者一心，同登仙道」。
t_en: A piece of folded letter that reads: "Mercy of Sanctus Medicus, the Arbor's new growth. Unity of Discipuli Medici, the Immortality's true path."

t_ch: 先拿走吧。
t_en: I'd better take this.

t_ch: （翻翻他的上衣内侧…）
t_en: (Let's check his jacket pockets...)

t_ch: 上衣内侧，裤兜里放不下的东西适合放在这里。
t_en: Inside the jacket. Things that cannot be put in the pants' pockets can be placed here.

t_ch: （钱包）
t_en: (Wallet)

t_ch: 仙舟上除却化外民少有人会持有钱包这样的东西。
t_en: Few people apart from the outworlders on Xianzhou would have a wallet.

t_ch: 你手中的钱包里没有钱，你在其中找到了一张叠好的照片。
t_en: No money has been found in this wallet. You can only see a folded photo inside of it.

t_ch: 先拿走吧。
t_en: I'd better take this.

t_ch: （再翻翻上衣内侧）
t_en: (Check his jacket pockets again...)

t_ch: 一张特殊的药方，涂涂改改，似乎和魔阴身有点关系……
t_en: A special but scratched-out prescription, which seems to have something to do with the Mara-struck.

t_ch: 先拿走吧。
t_en: I'd better take this.

t_ch: （脱下他的鞋子…）
t_en: (Let's take his shoes off...)

t_ch: 藏在鞋底，复古的藏物方式……
t_en: Hiding things in shoes... Such a classic method.

t_ch: （还是算了吧…）
t_en: (...or, maybe that's not necessary...)

t_ch: （就调查这么多好了。）
t_en: (I've done the checking.)

t_ch: 还没脱下此人的靴子，你感觉鼻尖仿佛在凑近一座闷烧的地狱……
t_en: Before taking off his shoes, you can feel that your nose is breathing fire from a burning hell...

t_ch: 敲门
t_en: Knock the door

t_ch: 这里没有人。
t_en: Nobody home.

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's me, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你回来找我们了，也就是安全的意思？
t_en: You're back! Does that mean we're safe now?

t_ch: 跟踪你们的人都已经处理了。
t_en: The person following you has been dealt with.

t_ch: 已经处理了，也就是安全的意思？
t_en: Dealt with...? Does that mean we're safe now?

t_ch: 我们马上出来。
t_en: We're coming out.

t_ch: 白露和半夏确定没有他人追上，从房间里走了出来。
t_en: After making sure they are not pursued anymore, Bailu and Banxia walk out of the room.

t_ch: 怎么样？那家伙果然是打算把本小姐抓回丹鼎司的吧，我早就猜到了。
t_en: What happened? That man wanted to catch me and take me back to the Alchemy Commission, right? I knew it!

t_ch: 你看，这位{F#少女}{M#少年}还是值得信任的吧？
t_en: See? I told you this {F#girl}{M#guy} was trustworthy.

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}确实帮助了我们，但我怕节外生枝…还是请回吧。
t_en: ...Thank you for helping us, but I'm worried you being here could just complicate matters further... Please leave us be.

t_ch: 刚才那人要找的人是你，半夏。
t_en: That man was looking for you, Banxia.

t_ch: 啊？他们找半夏姐姐干嘛，肯定是来抓我啦。
t_en: Huh? Why were they looking for Miss Banxia? That can't be right, they must've been looking for me.

t_ch: 我有异议！
t_en: I disagree!

t_ch: 我有证据！
t_en: I have evidence!

t_ch: 他在找与我同高的丹鼎司女性。
t_en: He was looking for an Alchemy Commission woman "about the same height" as me.

t_ch: …怎么可能。
t_en: ...How is that possible?

t_ch: （能够指出丹鼎司医士要找的人就是半夏，这样的证据是什么？）
t_en: (How can I prove the doctor from the Alchemy Commission was looking for Banxia?)

t_ch: 【药王秘传的信笺】
t_en: Disciples of Sanctus Medicus Letter

t_ch: 药王秘传的秘信，信中指明了「背叛药王秘传的医护半夏已经逃出了丹鼎司洞天」。
t_en: The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus letter indicates that Banxia, the nurse who betrayed the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, has already escaped the Alchemy Commission delve.

t_ch: 这就是证据！
t_en: Here's the proof!

t_ch: 【奇怪的药方】
t_en: Strange Prescription

t_ch: 奇奇怪怪的药方，好像没有提到任何有关半夏的信息。
t_en: A strange prescription, doesn't seem to be anything to do with Banxia

t_ch: （…现在还不知道药方跟半夏之间的关系，也许另有他用。）
t_en: (...Does this prescription really have anything to do with Banxia?)

t_ch: 【金色的徽章】
t_en: Golden Badge

t_ch: 这段证词，和这枚漂亮的徽章…没有产生什么矛盾。
t_en: The testimony doesn't seem at all at odds with the beautiful badge...

t_ch: 稍、稍等一下！我再找找。
t_en: W—Wait a minute! I'll keep looking.

t_ch: 【叠好的照片】
t_en: Folded Photo

t_ch: 只是一张普通的家庭合照，没有什么疑点。
t_en: An ordinary family photo, nothing to see here...

t_ch: 稍、稍等一下！我再找找。
t_en: W—Wait a minute! I'll keep looking.

t_ch: 这封信……！
t_en: This letter...!

t_ch: 等等，{NICKNAME}，我听说过「药王秘传」。
t_en: Wait, {NICKNAME}, I've heard of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 那些人都是崇拜魔阴身还有「寿瘟祸祖」的狂热信徒？可半夏不是这样的人。她…她还让我来治疗魔阴身呢。
t_en: Aren't they those crazy followers that worship the mara-struck and the Plagues Author? But Banxia's nothing like them... She... wanted me to treat the mara-struck.

t_ch: 白露大人！我们不是商量好了吗…这件事绝不能告诉外人。
t_en: Lady Bailu! You promised you wouldn't tell anyone about that!

t_ch: （「治疗魔阴身」很可疑，有进一步证明她是药王秘传的证据吗？）
t_en: (Wanted her to treat the mara-struck? Suspicious... Could she be a disciple of Sanctus Medicus? I wonder if I have any evidence...)

t_ch: 【药王秘传的信笺】
t_en: Disciples of Sanctus Medicus Letter

t_ch: 这封信已经证明了半夏与药王秘传存在关系，但不是决定性证据。
t_en: The letter has proven a link between Banxia and the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, but it's not decisive proof

t_ch: 稍、稍等一下！我再找找。
t_en: W—Wait a minute! I'll keep looking.

t_ch: 【奇怪的药方】
t_en: Strange Prescription

t_ch: 奇怪的药方，模糊的笔记上写着「延缓魔阴身」。
t_en: The strange prescription has a note in scribbled writing: "slowing the mara-struck process"

t_ch: 这是从药王秘传那里得到的药方，白露你能看看吗？
t_en: This is a prescription from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus. Bailu, can you take a look?

t_ch: 【金色的徽章】
t_en: Golden Badge

t_ch: 这段证词，和这枚漂亮的徽章…没有产生什么矛盾。
t_en: The testimony doesn't seem at all at odds with the beautiful badge...

t_ch: 稍、稍等一下！我再找找。
t_en: W—Wait a minute! I'll keep looking.

t_ch: 【叠好的照片】
t_en: Folded Photo

t_ch: 只是一张普通的家庭合照，没有什么疑点。
t_en: An ordinary family photo, nothing to see here...

t_ch: 稍、稍等一下！我再找找。
t_en: W—Wait a minute! I'll keep looking.

t_ch: 这个药方的字迹…和你交给我的那张一模一样。
t_en: The handwriting on this prescription is exactly the same as on the one you gave to me...

t_ch: 这么说来，你真是「药王秘传」的人？哼，半夏姐姐是个骗子！大骗子！
t_en: Banxia... are you really from the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus? Hmph, you're a liar! A great big liar!

t_ch: …抱歉，白露大人。
t_en: ...I'm sorry, Lady Bailu.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>我的确就是「药王秘传」的人。</color>那个丹鼎司的医士，也确实是他们派来抓捕我的。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I am a member of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus,</color> and that doctor from the Alchemy Commission was sent to catch me.

t_ch: 但我…我绝不是要伤害白露大人！恰恰相反，我带着她从那儿离开，也无形中保护了她的安全。
t_en: But I... I never meant to harm Lady Bailu! On the contrary, by taking her away from that place, I was protecting her.

t_ch: 出来之前，我和白露大人有一桩交易。这件事情非办到不可。求求你，请不要带走她！
t_en: I made a deal with Lady Bailu before we escaped — we have an urgent task to complete. Please, don't take her away!

t_ch: （先去问问白露的说法吧。）
t_en: (I should ask Bailu what she thinks about all this first.)

t_ch: 可恶。真是治伤治病治不了人心险恶啊…还以为有人真心实意帮我逃出丹鼎司。
t_en: Ugh... Treating wounds and diseases is one thing, but curing the evils of humanity...? There I was thinking someone helped me escape the Alchemy Commission because they genuinely wanted to help me.

t_ch: 罢了罢了，燥气郁心，不利长高。不跟她置气了。
t_en: Hmph, enough. Being angry will stunt my growth... Let's put this behind us.

t_ch: 你听说过「药王秘传」…
t_en: You've heard of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus...

t_ch: 我也只是听说过罢了。要不是在丹鼎司观颐台的卷牍里看过一些记载，「药王秘传」这四个字仙舟上知道的人怕也不多。
t_en: I don't know much. If it weren't for those scrolls in the Alchemy Commission's Elixir Research Terrace, I doubt anyone in the Xianzhou would've heard of them.

t_ch: 很久以前——有多久呢，对长生种来说都算的上很久了，仙舟把这个信奉「寿瘟祸祖」的组织连根拔起了。
t_en: A long time ago, even by the standards of long-life species, the Xianzhou eliminated an organization that worshiped the "Plagues Author."

t_ch: 要问为什么，大概是因为「药王秘传」这帮人脑子坏掉了，是真正意义上的脑子坏掉喔。
t_en: Why were they eliminated? Because they held an incredibly dangerous notion.

t_ch: 仙舟人想从魔阴身的宿命中解脱，重归恒常寂灭。但药王秘传却觉得魔阴身是赐福，是脱离肉身凡胎的进化。
t_en: Xianzhou people wanted to be released from the threat of mara and return to the stability of life and death. Yet the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus believed that being stricken with mara was a blessing — a natural evolutionary step in casting off the mortal coil.

t_ch: 话说得漂亮，不就是任自己变成没有人性的怪物吗？所以云骑军和十王司联手铲除了他们的教团。
t_en: Sounds great, right? Well, only if you think becoming a monster devoid of all humanity is a good thing. That's why the Cloud Knights and Ten-Lords Commission cooperated to eliminate the organization.

t_ch: 不过销声匿迹是一回事，传承断绝是另一回事。就跟医病一样，症候不显，才最麻烦。现在看来，这些人不仅没消失，还在暗中扩张哩。
t_en: But just because you can't see something doesn't mean it isn't there. It's like eradicating a sickness — easier to do when the symptoms are visible. In hindsight, not only did the Disciples endure, they expanded the organization in secret.

t_ch: 魔阴身到底是什么？
t_en: Just what does it mean to be mara-struck?

t_ch: 你问的问题，仙舟人自己也问了千百遍。
t_en: The people of the Xianzhou have asked themselves that question thousands of times.

t_ch: 数不清的医道智者都投身在破解这一诅咒的研究上。可惜，除了几个似是而非的答案之外，谁也没有结论。
t_en: Countless doctors and sages have dedicated their lives to researching a cure for the curse. Tragically, besides a few theories, no one has come to a definite conclusion.

t_ch: 只能说，「寿瘟祸祖」赐予的东西，血肉凡胎谁也参不透。
t_en: In truth, nobody in this world can fully comprehend the curse of the Plagues Author.

t_ch: 展开讲讲答案…
t_en: And the theories...?

t_ch: 有医士认为，仙舟人肉身不朽，但记忆却随岁月与日俱增。这种增长，最终会达到极限，脑袋里容不下更多记忆了。
t_en: Some doctors believe that because the people of the Xianzhou have immortal bodies, their memories accumulate until no more can be stored in the brain.

t_ch: 只有那些最鲜明、最极端的部分还能存在——通常不会是什么快乐的回忆，多是些后悔、痛恨、悲伤之类的负面积淀。
t_en: Only the most vivid and extreme memories persist... but such memories are usually ones of regret, hatred, grief, and other negative emotions.

t_ch: 「魔阴身」的征兆就是从此开始显现。不过，比这更可怕的还是之后肉身上的变化……
t_en: That's when the symptoms of being stricken with mara begin to show. But the physical changes are the most terrifying...

t_ch: 那你还要治疗魔阴身？
t_en: Then, why are you still treating the mara-struck?

t_ch: 出现魔阴身的仙舟人总是很早就被十王司的冥差判官带走了。我心想，若能在他们上路前，让他们不必那么痛苦，让他们能体面离开……
t_en: When symptoms strike, the mara-struck are quickly taken away by the spiritfarers and judges of the Ten-Lords Commission. I just want to reduce their suffering and let them leave with dignity...

t_ch: 不过，地衡司的人说的也没错……
t_en: But the people of the Realm-Keeping Commission do have a point...

t_ch: 丹鼎司和药王秘传有什么关系？
t_en: What's the connection between the Alchemy Commission and the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus?

t_ch: 这种事情，我怎么会知道啦。他们又不会在脸上写着「我是药王秘传」。
t_en: How should I know? It's not like commission members walk around with "Disciple of Sanctus Medicus" on their foreheads.

t_ch: 我没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 很抱歉，我没能对白露大人坦诚相告。但我有不得已的苦衷……
t_en: I'm sorry I wasn't honest with Lady Bailu, but I had no other choice...

t_ch: 你和龙女达成了什么交易？
t_en: What deal did you make with the Dragon Lady?

t_ch: 我想求龙女大人能救回我的爱人，所以，我用「带她逃离丹鼎司」作为交换条件……
t_en: I asked her to save my beloved. In return, I helped her escape from the Alchemy Commission...

t_ch: 虽然这样做触犯了持明族的律条，丹鼎司也一定会惩罚我。但我顾不着那么多了，我不能眼睁睁看着那人送命。所以……
t_en: I broke many of the Vidyadhara's laws, and the Alchemy Commission is bound to punish me, but I just can't let my beloved die...

t_ch: 我还有一些问题想要问你…
t_en: I still have some questions for you...

t_ch: 我剩下的时间已不多了，但你问吧……
t_en: I don't have much time left, but go ahead...

t_ch: 你为什么要瞒着白露？
t_en: Why did you hide all this from Bailu?

t_ch: 我本以为只要逃出来便没事了…没想到那些人还是追来了。
t_en: I thought we'd be fine after we escaped... I didn't know those people would come after us.

t_ch: 加入「药王秘传」这种事，还有在其中经历的种种，我实在没法对那个孩子开口……
t_en: I just couldn't find a way to tell her I was a disciple... and about all the things I experienced there...

t_ch: 你虽然对我很好，但却不该瞒着我。最讨厌大人说一半藏一半，要东猜西猜，太头疼哩。
t_en: You've been nice to me, but you shouldn't have kept me in the dark. I hate when grownups hide secrets and only tell you half the story — it's such a headache.

t_ch: 关于你的心上人…？
t_en: Who is your beloved...?

t_ch: 他叫梁沐，是个来求药的短生种。
t_en: His name is Liangmu. He's a short-life species that requested an elixir.

t_ch: 我原本觉得，短生种短如弹指般的性命，又何必再苦苦挣扎。但看到他忍着病痛坚持接受治疗的样子，我的心也跟着揪紧起来了。
t_en: I used to think that the suffering of short-life species was nothing. But my heart... my heart ached when I saw him in such pain.

t_ch: 那种感受又甜蜜，又痛苦。我这辈子从来没体会过，我没想过自己会爱上一个短生种。
t_en: It's something I never expected to experience in my lifetime — falling in love with a short-life species... such a bittersweet feeling.

t_ch: 于是我想到了白露大人，凡人腠疾，龙女垂泪便足以医治。可是能让白露大人亲自问诊的病历已经排到三十年以后了。
t_en: That's when I thought of Lady Bailu — the Dragon Lady that can heal mortals through tears alone. But it would take <unbreak>30</unbreak> years for her to treat all those in need.

t_ch: 我只能出此下策，大人，原谅我……
t_en: I had no choice but to come up with this plan. Please forgive me...

t_ch: 你说时间不多了？
t_en: You said you didn't have much time left?

t_ch: 我已有堕入魔阴身的征兆了。
t_en: I'm already showing symptoms of the mara-struck...

t_ch: 「药王秘传」视魔阴身为「登仙」，常常服食各种怪异的方子加以催化。我也不例外。
t_en: The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus regard the mara-struck as saints — they take all kinds of strange medicines to speed up the process... I was no exception.

t_ch: 可自从认识了梁沐，我突然觉得，登仙是件很可怕的事。就算不老不死，要是不认得他，不记得他，或者变成了他不认识的样子……
t_en: But since I met Liangmu, I've realized that these "saints" are horrible creatures. Even if I were to become immortal, to not recognize him, to forget him or become something that he doesn't recognize...

t_ch: 所以我停服了丹方，逃走了。这一路上多亏了龙女大人的帮助，我才能安然走到这里。
t_en: That's why I stopped taking the medicines and escaped. Thanks to the Dragon Lady, I made it here safely.

t_ch: 要问的就这么多了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to ask.

t_ch: 白露，你想跟她一起走吗？
t_en: Bailu, do you want to go with her?

t_ch: 哼，虽然她有事瞒着我。但我毕竟答应了她，要去救她的心上人。
t_en: Hmph, she hid everything from me... but I promised her I'd save her beloved.

t_ch: 看了这么多年诊，还是不习惯见到有人受苦哩。
t_en: After all these years, I'm still not used to seeing people suffer.

t_ch: 白露大人……
t_en: Lady Bailu...

t_ch: 半夏，说说接下来你想做什么吧。
t_en: Banxia, tell us what you want to do next.

t_ch: 直接说吧，你想要我做什么？
t_en: Cut to the chase, what do you want me to do?

t_ch: 我不想被「药王秘传」或者十王司的人盘查。我们得找个法子，将我们不引人瞩目地送到流云渡去。
t_en: I can't let the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus or the Ten-Lords Commission find us. We need to find a way to get to Cloudford without drawing their attention.

t_ch: 我想到了一个人。
t_en: I have someone in mind.

t_ch: 在下是地衡司勤务广大。有急事的话，请到公廨内部…啊，这不是衔药龙女大人吗？
t_en: I'm Guangda, an orderly from the Realm-Keeping Commission. If you have an urgent issue, please go to the chancery... Huh? Aren't you the Healer Lady?
Shh!

t_ch: 我们有事要找你。
t_en: We want to ask you a favor.

t_ch: 见着龙女大人，还不下跪。
t_en: Kneel before the Dragon Lady.

t_ch: 仙舟上哪有这种陈规旧俗啊。
t_en: Hah, the Xianzhou did away with all that nonsense eras ago.

t_ch: {F#大姐}{M#大哥}你小声点儿！
t_en: Keep it down!

t_ch: 如果是公事公办的话，请到公廨内部，大毫、松烟他们应该就在里面…我只是一个小小勤务，涉及龙女大人的事情，只能说是爱莫能助。
t_en: If it's official business you're here about, you'll need to go to the chancery — Dahao and Songyan should be there. I'm just an orderly... As for anything concerning the Dragon Lady, I'm sorry, but there's nothing I can do.

t_ch: 你误会了，不是我，而是我的一个朋友。明白吗？我的朋友有星槎通行许可证，能帮你们速速离开…不是我啊，这个人不是我。
t_en: You've got it wrong. That's not my ship, it's my friend's. My friend has a starskiff travel permit and can help you leave quickly... I'm not the one who owns the ship.

t_ch: 少啰嗦，我就问你是可以走、还是不可以走？
t_en: Just cut to the chase. Can we take the ship or not?

t_ch: 可以走、可以走，小声点儿。只要价格到位，我都能送…啊不对，是我那位朋友都能送，各位放心好了。怎么样，现在就出发吗？
t_en: Yes, you can. but please keep it down! I can take you there if the price is right... Uh, I mean, my friend can take you there... so relax. How about it? Shall we set off?

t_ch: 出发。（前往流云渡）
t_en: Let's go. (Go to Cloudford)

t_ch: 你们先去流云渡，我之后过来。
t_en: Go to Cloudford. I'll catch up with you later.

t_ch: …病情不等人，我们就只等一会儿，一会儿。
t_en: ...I don't have long. We'll wait for a while... just a short while.

t_ch: 我想搭乘你的船去流云渡…
t_en: I'd like to take your ship to Cloudford...

t_ch: 怎么样，现在就出发吗？
t_en: How about it? Shall we set off?

t_ch: 我还有别的事情，稍等
t_en: Not now, I have other things to do.

t_ch: 怎么样，现在就出发吗？
t_en: How about it? Shall we set off?

t_ch: 孩子望向海水，口中哼哼着谁也听不懂的童谣。
t_en: The child gazes back at the sea, humming a nursery rhyme that nobody can understand.

t_ch: 他偶尔察觉到你的存在，好奇地看向你，但很快却无法集中注意力，又被奇异的景观夺去了心神。
t_en: Every now and again, he seems to notice you and he looks on with curiosity. But before long, he can no longer concentrate as he's distracted by the fantastical landscape.

t_ch: 大海似在呼唤着孩子。
t_en: The sea seems to be calling for the child.

t_ch: 一道身影伫立在此，遥望着远方的雕像，一言不发。
t_en: A figure is standing here, completely silent as it gazes at a statue in the distance.

t_ch: …是你啊。
t_en: ...It's you.

t_ch: 是我。
t_en: It's me.

t_ch: 怎么是你？
t_en: It's you!?

t_ch: 男人一语不发地看着你。
t_en: The guy stares at you in silence.

t_ch: 你好，阿刃。
t_en: Hello, Bladie.

t_ch: 下次再这么称呼我，我会用剑回答。
t_en: Call me that again and I'll be answering you with my sword.

t_ch: 艾利欧对你另有安排，所以你可以活着离开了。
t_en: Elio has other plans for you, so you may leave here alive.

t_ch: 你在做什么？
t_en: What are you doing?

t_ch: 你为什么在这里？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: …悼念愚行。
t_en: ...Mourning for folly.

t_ch: 那个男人，他得到满足了么？
t_en: That guy, was he happy?

t_ch: 我想是的。
t_en: I think so.

t_ch: 我想没有。
t_en: I don't think so.

t_ch: 人终有一死，有的人含恨而终，有的人能够寻得自己的埋骨之地。
t_en: Everybody dies. Some people die harboring hatred, while others get to visit their own burial site.

t_ch: 可我却只能活着，无休无止地活着。
t_en: But I can only live, and keep living on, endlessly forever.

t_ch: 男人长叹一声，不再看你。
t_en: The man lets out a lengthy sigh and stops looking at you.

t_ch: 一个孩子孤零零地站在陶德时常等待的地方，不知为何，他稚嫩的面庞有些眼熟。
t_en: A child stands alone in the place where Todd was always waiting. For some reason, his young face looks rather familiar.

t_ch: 你来了啊。
t_en: There you are.

t_ch: 你怎么变成这副模样了？
t_en: How did you turn into... this?

t_ch: 到底发生什么事了？
t_en: What in the world happened?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，一次草率的实验，一个必然的结果。
t_en: Hehe, I just was a little sloppy with my experiment. An inevitable result.

t_ch: 我本来以为自己已经获得「长生」了，可惜，事情并没有这么简单。
t_en: I thought I'd gained immortality, but unfortunately things were not that simple.

t_ch: 我的体形在不断缩小。皮肤、肌肉、内脏…我确实越来越年轻了，只是这份「青春」似乎没有尽头。
t_en: My body, it keeps shrinking. My skin, muscles, organs... Indeed, I'm getting younger, you see, but this "youth" doesn't seem to have an off switch.

t_ch: 返老还童？
t_en: You're de-aging?

t_ch: 有点像持明族的蜕生…
t_en: Kind of like the Vidyadhara tradition of hatching rebirth...

t_ch: 是啊，不过据我了解，他们的「蜕生」是有极限的。
t_en: Yes. But so far as I know, their "rebirths" have a limit.

t_ch: 从测试结果看，这个逆向生长的过程不会停止。要不了多久，我就会再也认不得字，看不懂自己写的报告，这和老年痴呆似乎没什么不同。
t_en: Test results indicate that this de-aging process seems to have no endpoint. It won't be long before I'm illiterate and won't be able to understand my own reports — which would seem no difference from regular dementia.

t_ch: 最终我也许会像持明一样变成一枚卵，也许会重新成为人类的胚胎——毕竟我并不是持明族。
t_en: In the end, I might even become an egg again, just like the Vidyadhara. Perhaps I could become a human embryo once again — I mean, I'm not Vidyadhara, am I?

t_ch: 啊…真是个讽刺的结果，不是吗？
t_en: Ahh... Isn't it ironic? Don't you think?

t_ch: 我实现了愿望，只是它不是以我所想的形式。人类最古老的故事都是这么个调调。现在看来，这些陈腔滥调也不无道理。
t_en: My wish came true — just not in the form I thought it would take. The oldest stories in human civilization are fables that take this line. Seems like they aren't just a cliche, after all.

t_ch: 需要我帮你做些什么？
t_en: What can I help you with?

t_ch: 你已经为我做了很多很多了。比我的老师和弟子们加起来都多。而我已不需要再麻烦任何人了。
t_en: You've already done so much for me. More than my teachers and apprentices combined. And I don't want to bother anyone else anymore.

t_ch: 需要我请丹鼎司的医生吗？
t_en: Do you want me to fetch a healer from the Alchemy Commission?
